description: LTX-2.3 22B video FACE / HEAD SWAP using Alissonerdx's 'BFS Best Face Swap' V3 head-swap LoRA (rank-adaptive). You give it a target/body video (the motion + scene) and a face reference image (the identity); the swap follows the body video's motion, lighting and audio while taking identity from the face image. V3 uses a 'persistent-template' guide: the reference face is composited into a reserved chroma-key side strip on every frame (ReservedRegionFrameComposer) so the identity signal never drifts. Graph: Alissonerdx/BFS-Best-Face-Swap-Video 'workflow_ltx2_head_swap_drag_and_drop_v3.0', derived for Spark by presets/ltx_faceswap.derive.py.

docs.label: BFS Best Face Swap model card

about.what: Swaps the face/head in a video for the person in a reference photo — keeping the original performance, motion, lighting and audio.
about.inputs: A body/motion video (kept as-is) and one face reference photo (the new identity to swap in).
about.key_knobs: Face reference photo — a square-ish, well-lit, front-on shot works best. The FACE/ACTION prompt — describe the reference face's traits, and the performer only as 'a person'. Head-swap strength — 1.0 is the baseline.

_HOWITRUNS: No-build path (same as the other presets): the public yanwk image runs comfy_run.py, which clones node_packs, downloads the 6 weights onto the Spark node, converts ltx_faceswap.workflow.json (UI graph) -> API via /object_info, applies patches, optionally splices a Tier-1 --lora stack onto the head-swap LoRA (anchor 573), and renders. ALL weights are pinned/verified, so unlike the IC-LoRA presets this is NOT a template — it can run as-is. ~30 GB is fetched on a COLD start (use --idle-hold to keep a warm node). --convert-only skips the model pull and validates the converted graph cheaply.
_DERIVED: The shipped workflow is NOT the raw Alisson graph. presets/ltx_faceswap.derive.py flattens it for Spark (the raw graph can't convert as-is): (1) flattens ~68 KJNodes Set/Get virtual nodes into direct links — comfy_run's converter has no Set/Get resolution; (2) reconnects through the bypassed alt-LoRA loaders (419/537) onto the head-swap LoRA; (3) rewires the final text encode straight from the 'Positive Prompt' box (498), dropping the Ollama auto-prompt path (OllamaVideoDescriber hits localhost:11434, which doesn't exist on Spark); (4) bypasses the abliterated-gemma CLIP LoRA (424, weight not pinned — re-enable + add its weight for uncensored prompts); (5) mutes the custom-audio loader + the 2 comparison-preview combiners + the dead upscaler loader, leaving node 341 SaveVideo as the single output. Re-run the derive if the upstream graph moves.
_MODELNOTE: The UNETLoader is pointed at the EXACT build Alisson's V3 graph specifies: Kijai's 'ltx-2.3-22b-distilled_transformer_only_fp8_input_scaled_v3' (Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy — the same repo our other weights come from). This was originally substituted with 'distilled-1.1_..._fp8_scaled' on the wrong assumption that the input_scaled_v3 build was private; it's public, and matching it matters — a head-swap LoRA is fit to its base weights, so the revision (base distilled vs 1.1) and quant (input_scaled vs scaled) both affect motion/likeness. The head-swap LoRA is the V3 rank-adaptive (fro-0.98) file; the V3 rank_64 variant is the alternative.
_AUDIO: LTX-2.3 is audio-aware: the active audio rides the BODY VIDEO's own audio track (node 345 -> the AV-latent path -> CreateVideo). A clip WITH audio gives the best lip motion; a silent clip may need the audio path muted. There is no separate audio input flag — supply audio in the body video.
_GUIDE_MASK: V3 persistent-template (ReservedRegionFrameComposer, node 360): the face image is composited into a green chroma-key strip on the left, 256px, every frame (chroma is internal only — the final video is the original framing). Per the model card: a SQUARE-ish, well-lit, frontal/3-4 face reference gives the most stable identity; LoRA strength 1.0 is the baseline (>1.0 = stronger identity/hair capture but can distort motion). Mask/face quality matters more than raising strength.

ui.title: LTX-2.3 Face Swap — swap a face/head into a video from a reference image

primary_input.label: Body / target video
primary_input.tooltip: The clip whose motion, scene, lighting and AUDIO are kept. The face/head is replaced with the reference image's identity. LTX-2.3 is audio-aware — a clip WITH an audio track gives the best lip motion. Output orientation follows this clip.

secondary_input.label: Face reference image
secondary_input.tooltip: The identity to swap IN. Use a SQUARE-ish, well-lit, frontal or 3/4 face shot for the most stable identity (the V3 'persistent template' composites it into every frame). One clear face works best.

prompt.default:
head_swap:

FACE:
The person from the reference image.

ACTION:
A person faces the camera and moves naturally, matching the original performance.

ui.label: Prompt (FACE / ACTION)
ui.tooltip: V3 uses a STRUCTURED prompt. Keep the 'head_swap:' header, then FACE: (objective identity traits of the reference face — gender, ethnicity, skin tone, age range, hair, eye color, facial hair, distinctive features) and ACTION: (describe the performer only as 'a person' — body movement, clothing, hand actions, framing, environment; do NOT describe the original face). (src: BFS V3 model card)

negative.default: pc game, console game, video game, cartoon, childish, ugly, artifacts, low resolution, blurry, jagged edges

ui.label: Negative prompt
ui.tooltip: The negative shipped in Alisson's V3 graph. Trim if results soften.

ui.label: Head-swap strength
ui.tooltip: Head-swap LoRA strength. 1.0 is the recommended baseline (best motion fidelity). >1.0 captures identity/hair harder but can distort the original motion; lower if the body/motion warps. Mask + face-reference quality matter more than raising this. (src: BFS V3 model card)

ui.label: Base resolution (largest side)
ui.tooltip: The longest output dimension; the other side follows the body clip's aspect. 768 is what V2/V3 were trained at and what the model card recommends for inference — going higher (e.g. 1024) pushes the model off-distribution and tends to melt the frame. Best framing is close-up / landscape. Divisible by 32. (src: BFS V3 model card — '768 is the recommended inference resolution')

ui.label: Frame rate (fps)
ui.tooltip: Drives both the body-clip load rate and the output fps. Match your source for natural motion. LTX-2.3 ceiling is 50fps. (src: LTX-2.3 docs)

ui.label: Duration (seconds)
ui.tooltip: Length of the swap in seconds (frames = duration x fps, capped to the body clip). LTX-2.3 max ~10s @50fps or ~20s @25fps. (src: LTX-2.3 docs)

ui.label: Sampler steps
ui.tooltip: Denoising steps for the distilled transformer (bong_tangent schedule). 8 is the tuned baseline. If the swap looks rough or the mouth jitters, try 12-16 — more steps = cleaner motion/detail at higher cost, with diminishing returns past ~20 on a distilled model. CLI: --set 575.inputs.steps <n>.

ui.label: Seed
ui.tooltip: Random seed. Change it to roll a different result when a take looks cursed; keep it fixed to reproduce one. Same seed + same inputs = same output. CLI: --set 377.inputs.noise_seed <n>.
